Disassembly diff vs target confirms WIP's structural-ceiling claim exactly: idx 0 `move a2,a0` (mine) vs `move a3,a0` (target) ‚Äî X-preserve register, cc1 ascending-allocator picks the lower-numbered $a2 ‚Äî then cascades through ~12 register-rotation diffs including the missing target idx 16 `move a3,v0` park-merge. No tested structural perturbation flips this without invoking forbidden constructs (literal renames, UB-conditional-init, dead-conditional store). sessions.lever: workflow round 2: BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG (tmp/gccdbg/cc1) instrumented cc1 dump on score-12 candidate AND sibling func_8007C748 (matched 3-arg) AND func_8007C938 (matched 2-arg dispatch sibling) + 13 fresh structural variants
sessions.note: ALLOCDBG MECHANISM EVIDENCE: at score-12 form, cc1 emits 11 pseudos. The X-preserve pseudo is #72 (nrefs=2 livelen=11 pri=1818, ord=10 LAST) and gets $a2 (reg 6). Target wants it in $a3 (reg 7). KEY FINDING: variant v6 (`s32 lim_x = D_8009BE78` block-local) IS THE FIRST KNOWN LEVER that flips the X-preserve to $a3 ‚Äî ALLOCDBG confirms a new pseudo 91 (nrefs=3 livelen=7 pri=4285) gets $a2 at ord=6, and pseudo 76 (X-preserve role, ord=8) flips to $a3. v6 also produces target's missing idx-16 `move a3,v0` park-merge. BUT v6 scores 18 (REGRESSION) because lim_x's lifetime forces an extra early `move $3,$4` preserve and shifts pseudo 77 from $a2 to $a1, netting +6 cascade diffs. Three additional ALLOCDBG takeaways: (1) C748 has only 5 pseudos, args take $a0/$a1/$a2 naturally ‚Äî fundamentally different shape; (2) C938 has only 4 pseudos and uses `new_var2 = arg0` as SOTN-allowed named-intermediate (works because no X-clamp clobbers $a0); (3) X-preserve in C86C is structurally necessary (target's idx-12-14 reuses $a0 for `lim_x-1` then re-reads preserve in branch delay slot at idx 14 `addu v0, a3, $zero`). Mechanism for flipping the X-preserve to $a3 NOW CHARACTERIZED: introduce pseudo with priority > pseudo-72 but lifetime/refs that DON'T also bias pseudo 76/77's allocation. v6 is the existence proof. The score-12 candidate remains the practical floor under explored named-intermediate / decl-order / operand-flip lever class. v17 (v6 + no widemask precompute) scored 23 = worst regression.

sessions.note: All 12 variants either fold back to candidate's score-12 form (combine-foldable shapes ‚Äî v23 s16 lim_x at 12) or match v6's score-18 form (cost-equivalent shapes with the same lifetime-induced cascade ‚Äî v19, v20, v21, v22, v26, v28 all at 18) or regress further (v18 at 14, v24 at 21, v25 at 22, v27 at 20, v29 at 22). CONCLUSION: every variant that introduces a separate pseudo for lim_x (s32-width, livelen > 0) emits the v6 cost (+6 cascade). Every variant that folds back to no-separate-pseudo (s16 lim_x, lim_x referenced once before combine) matches candidate's score 12. There is no middle ground in the explored shape space. This is the same v0 + +1insn-trap that defeats cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec siblings ‚Äî documented 'lever-class structural ceiling' per register-alloc-pure-c sessions 7-10. sessions.lever: session 5 (NO_PROGRESS): PRIORITY 3 diagnostic ‚Äî `register s16 x_pin asm("a3") = arg0;` + body uses x_pin in X-clamp
sessions.note: PIN DIAGNOSTIC RESULT: score-12 unchanged. cheat_asm_stripped count rose 452‚Üí453 (sandbox stripped the pin block before scoring; pin is unconditionally score-inert per the cheat-invisible sandbox contract). This is decisive new evidence for the PARK_CANDIDATE escalation: even when GCC is instructed to put arg0 in $a3 via the pin, the post-strip residual stays at 12 ‚Äî meaning the cascade cost ISN'T driven by 'wrong register for the X-preserve pseudo alone.' Round 2's v6 lever (score 18) achieved the $a3 flip by introducing a NEW pseudo (lim_x) that shifted the WHOLE allocno priority order; that all-pseudos shift is what makes $a3 the natural choice AND simultaneously cascades pseudo 77 to $a1 (the +6 cost). A pin only directs ONE pseudo's allocation; the rest of the cascade remains. The honest pure-C path to score 0 must therefore either (a) flip pseudo 72 to $a3 WITHOUT introducing any new pseudo (which appears structurally impossible under combine-foldable C ‚Äî every tested transform either folds away with no priority effect, or introduces a pseudo that triggers the cascade), or (b) accept the cascade exists structurally and search for a pure-C source for the post-cascade allocation (the 12-diff register-rotation set itself). Lever search space considered exhausted under the explored class. ESCALATION: same family + identical structural ceiling as cpu_side_move_dir_4, marionation_Exec, saEft00Add, sibling func_8007C7A0. Per [[no-new-park-categories]] the standing options are (i) further directed permuter from score-12 base (untried at this session; budget-constrained ‚Äî PRIORITY 2 ALLOCDBG dump already done in round 2 and characterized the mechanism), (ii) global rodata reorder cluster decision shared with cpu_side_move_dir_4 ‚Äî USER POLICY decision, (iii) hand-coded-asm canonical authorization ‚Äî but C86C is structurally normal C (X/Y clamp + mode dispatch); zero hand-coded-asm signals (no GTE ops, no scratchpad, no syscalls, no canonical sign-split). Recommended escalation: surface to user as a CONFIRMED instance of the marionation_Exec / cpu_side_move_dir_4 / C7A0 register-rotation cluster; user judgment needed on whether to (a) keep parked indefinitely under existing INCOMPLETE state pending future toolchain insight, or (b) authorize the joint global rodata reorder decision.

sessions.note:
Session 7 PARK_CANDIDATE-criteria-completion run. Reproduced documented score-12 candidate floor at HEAD 2ef922c1 (build_insns 50 vs target 51, 21 rules dropped, 452 cheat-asm stripped). Four NEW structural variants distinct from rejected_forms tested, all NEGATIVE:
  N1 (`s32 mode_minus_one = D_8009BE74 - 1;` block-local before dispatch, then `if ((u32)mode_minus_one >= 2U)`): score 12 (flat). Mode-flag block-local has zero priority effect ‚Äî combine eliminates the named intermediate before allocno priority calc; equivalent to direct inline read.
  N2 (drop `r_e4` named intermediate, inline `return var_v1 | var_v0 | 0xE4000000`): score 12 (flat). The OR-chain associativity collapse via combine canonicalization is allocation-neutral ‚Äî r_e4 not load-bearing for floor 12 (in either present or absent form).
  N3 (unsigned-comparison combined sign+limit: `if ((u32)arg0 < (u32)D_8009BE78) var_v0_2 = arg0; else if (arg0 < 0) var_v0_2 = 0; else var_v0_2 = D_8009BE78 - 1;` on both X and Y clamps): score 37 (worst regression). Branchless-equivalent encoding via single unsigned compare moves codegen to entirely different shape ‚Äî emits sltu + nested branch tree, scheduling cascade.
  N4 (`s32 mode = D_8009BE74;` UNCONDITIONALLY at function TOP, before var_a1 preload ‚Äî extended-lifetime variant of N1): score 20 (regression). Top-level read extends mode's pseudo lifetime across the entire clamp region; bumps its allocno priority enough to shift another pseudo's allocation, regressing 8 points.
FRESH PERMUTER RUN: ran tools/decomp-permuter/permuter.py on permuter/c86c (-j4, 10min timeout, base score 225 weighted) ‚Äî 1191 iterations completed. Sandbox-measured: 130-1 IS a forbidden cheat-by-spelling ‚Äî it sets `var_a1 = arg1;` unconditionally at function top THEN overwrites `var_a1 = 0` in the arg0<0 else branch. The unconditional preload is a DEAD STORE on the arg0<0 path (overwritten before any read). Same family as func_8007B844's directed-permuter find (forbidden 2026-06-01, [[no-new-park-categories]] confirmed example ‚Äî 'inner v = x; is a dead conditional store ‚Äî same intent as the forbidden arg0 = 0; Lever D'). Vetted per the 6-test checklist: (1) contains dead store on arg0<0 path = YES, (2) human programmer would NOT naturally write the unconditional preload + conditional overwrite combo, (3) justification cites RA priority effect (var_a1's first-pseudo-via-arg1 store influencing allocno numbering) = YES, (4) 'necessary only because permuter found it lowers score' = YES exactly. REJECTED.

Future joint-cluster effort should focus exclusively on the allocation-priority axis with C7A0/B844/CBB0 cross-port lever search; the single-function PRIODBG/SCHEDDBG axis is empirically exhausted for C86C.

CANONICAL-ASM AUTHORIZATION IS NOT APPLICABLE: C86C is structurally normal C (X/Y clamp + mode dispatch); zero hand-coded signals (no GTE ops, no scratchpad pokes, no syscalls, no sign-split, no canonical strength-reduction-defeat) per [[hand-coded-asm-recognition]] / [[packed-multiply-cluster]]. JOINT WITH C7A0: identical mechanism + identical structural ceiling; any user-policy decision applies to both simultaneously.

Workflow round 3 (2026-06-03) outcomes ‚Äî PRIORITY 1: Lever D-style chain-extender is FORBIDDEN per 2026-06-02 rule on cpu_side_move_dir_4's combine-foldable chain-extender; this avenue is closed. PRIORITY 2: BB2_PRIO_DEBUG instrumented cc1 dump on candidate vs v6 (tmp/gccdbg/cc1 referenced in register-alloc-pure-c.md session 7) ‚Äî would reveal which dependency edges create v6's extra `move $3, $4` preserve at idx 0; UNTRIED at C86C specifically (round-2 ALLOCDBG was the global.c priority hook, not PRIODBG/sched). PRIORITY 3 [DONE session 5]: register asm pin diagnostic ‚Äî confirmed cascade is structural (pin score-inert).

Workflow round 2 (2026-06-02) v6 finding (`s32 lim_x = D_8009BE78` block-local) is the FIRST KNOWN LEVER that flips C86C's X-preserve pseudo to target's $a3 ‚Äî but it costs +6 score points due to (a) early `move $3,$4` and (b) pseudo 77 cascade to $a1. Next session target: ELIMINATE those costs via (1) PERMUTER FROM v6 BASE ‚Äî directed PERM_GENERAL on prologue region from the score-18 lim_x form, ~30k iters, vet output against cheat catalog (no UB-conditional-init, no dead-conditional-store, no param-aliases); OR (2) MICRO-VARIANT SWEEP ON LIM_X SHAPE ‚Äî try (i) lim_x declared outer-scope but only read in if-branch, (ii) lim_x = `s16` (vs s32 ‚Äî test if sign-extension penalty can be bypassed), (iii) lim_x = `(s32)D_8009BE78` explicit cast, (iv) lim_x = `D_8009BE78 - 1` directly, (v) lim_x read once for comparison only with `var_v0_2 = D_8009BE78 - 1` (replacing lim_x - 1 in the assignment). The mechanism dump shows pseudo 91 emerges in v6; the question is whether a different lim_x shape produces an EQUIVALENT pseudo 91 without the early preserve emit. ALSO: run BB2_PRIO_DEBUG on candidate vs v6 to verify which dependency edges create v6's extra preserve insn ‚Äî points at the exact RTL-level chain to break in C source. JOINT WITH C7A0: iterate v6 finding on C7A0 too (constant 0xE3 vs 0xE4 is the only difference ‚Äî should produce SAME ALLOCDBG flip).

Joint instrumented `BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG` cc1 dump of func_8007C86C at the score-12 form + sibling func_8007C748 (3-arg, matched pure-C) ‚Äî same as the cpu_side_move_dir_4 session 5 lever discovery method. The dump exposes the priority gap between the pseudo getting $a2 (mine) vs the equivalent in C748 that achieves a natural higher-callee-save landing. If C748's allocation table shows a specific livelen/refs profile that flips the tiebreaker, search for a combine-foldable pure-C lever that mimics it in C86C without emitting bytes (analogous to C5MD4's tbl_125c+delta chain). Also worth checking: ALLOCDBG with the Y wide-mask precompute already applied (i.e. the score-12 form), since prior sessions only ran it on the score-17 clean v8 base.
tools/decomp-permuter from the score-12 base (NOT the score-17 base session 3 used). Directed at the X-preserve register slot. CRITICAL: invoke cheat-reviewer on output.
ALLOCDBG-instrumented dump on the score-12 form to identify cc1's $a2-vs-$a3 tiebreaker mechanism.
Sibling func_8007C748 (3-arg variant, matched pure-C) .greg cross-reference.
Joint lever-search with C7A0 ‚Äî the two functions share the structural ceiling exactly; a lever found for one will apply to the other.

rejected_forms.form: Round 10 T1 (v2 Lever 2 block-local precompute): move `var_v1 = var_a1 & 0xFFF;` UNCONDITIONAL precompute from before dispatch INTO the else (wide-mode) arm only ‚Äî single-set pseudo confined to wide arm; narrow arm unchanged.
rejected_forms.score_note: build_insns 51 matches target's 51 (vs candidate's 50). EXACT magnitude match with C7A0 round-9 T1 (+6).
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+6 vs candidate). The shortened var_v1 livelen widens the masked-Lev cascade rather than narrowing it; the precompute hoisted ABOVE dispatch was load-bearing for the score-12 floor's specific X-preserve assignment pattern. Disproves the SOTN-duplicate-read-can-be-narrower hypothesis from v2 research. Cross-port: C7A0 round-9 T1 = 18 (+6), C86C round-10 T1 = 18 (+6) ‚Äî IDENTICAL. (Workflow round 10, 2026-06-05, git_head 07635825.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 10 T2 (v2 Lever 1 mode hoist): declare `s32 mode_m1 = D_8009BE74 - 1;` at function entry, use `mode_m1` in dispatch `if ((u32)mode_m1 >= 2U)` instead of inline `D_8009BE74 - 1`.
rejected_forms.score_note: build_insns 48 (3 short vs target 51). EXACT magnitude match with C7A0 round-9 T2 (+11).
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+11 vs candidate). GCC CSEs mode_m1 back to the inline form AND the byte-global early read shifts allocation in the wrong direction (build_insns drops to 48). The pseudo-priority rebalance the v2 mechanism predicted doesn't materialize. Cross-port: C7A0 round-9 T2 = 23 (+11), C86C round-10 T2 = 23 (+11) ‚Äî IDENTICAL. (Workflow round 10, 2026-06-05, git_head 07635825.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 10 T3 (v2 Lever 5 arg1_neg sign hoist): `s32 arg1_neg = arg1 < 0;` precomputed at entry, then Y-clamp predicate uses `if (!arg1_neg)` instead of `if (var_a1 >= 0)`.
rejected_forms.score_note: build_insns 51 matches target. EXACT magnitude match with C7A0 round-9 T3 (+16).
rejected_forms.reason: Severe regression (+16 vs candidate). The sign-extend pseudo target.s emits at idx 14-15 is NOT synthesizable via a comparison-result hoist ‚Äî the comparison RTL is materially different from the sign-extend insn pair target emits, and target's pseudo dies at the bltz check while the hoisted `arg1_neg` survives across the X-clamp. Cross-port: C7A0 round-9 T3 = 28 (+16), C86C round-10 T3 = 28 (+16) ‚Äî IDENTICAL. (Workflow round 10, 2026-06-05, git_head 07635825.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 10 T4 (v2 Lever 4 pointer share): `s16 *limits = &D_8009BE78;` at entry, then `limits[0] - 1` (X-limit) and `limits[1] - 1` (Y-limit) instead of direct globals.
rejected_forms.score_note: build_insns 50 matches candidate. EXACT magnitude match with C7A0 round-9 T4 (+6).
rejected_forms.reason: Regression (+6 vs candidate). CSE collapses both `limits[0]` and `limits[1]` LO16 references back to the same shape as direct global access, but the introduced pointer pseudo's livelen costs the masked-Lev cascade. Same outcome as round-7 T1's lim_x save ‚Äî the pointer-arithmetic vs scalar-arithmetic distinction the v2 lever predicted is invisible to cc1's RTL after combine. Cross-port: C7A0 round-9 T4 = 18 (+6), C86C round-10 T4 = 18 (+6) ‚Äî IDENTICAL. (Workflow round 10, 2026-06-05, git_head 07635825.)

rejected_forms.form: Round 10 T5 (v2 Lever 3 combined): apply Lever 1 (mode_m1 hoist) + Lever 2 (var_v1 precompute in else only) + drop r_e4 named local (`return var_v1 | var_v0 | 0xE4000000;`) simultaneously.
rejected_forms.score_note: build_insns 48 (3 short). EXACT magnitude match with C7A0 round-9 T5 (+17).
rejected_forms.reason: Severe regression (+17 vs candidate). The individually-negative levers (T2 +11, T1 +6) compound their regressions rather than cancelling. Per session-5/8 ALLOCDBG findings cited in the v2 research, fewer pseudos CAN flip the sort, but only if the removed pseudos were the ones biasing AGAINST target's choice ‚Äî in this case they were biasing TOWARD target's choice. Cross-port: C7A0 round-9 T5 = 29 (+17), C86C round-10 T5 = 29 (+17) ‚Äî IDENTICAL. (Workflow round 10, 2026-06-05, git_head 07635825.)

rejected_forms.form: N1 (session 7, 2026-06-04): `s32 mode_minus_one = D_8009BE74 - 1;` block-local before dispatch
rejected_forms.reason: Flat. Mode-flag block-local has zero priority effect ‚Äî combine eliminates the named intermediate before allocno priority calc; equivalent to direct inline read.

rejected_forms.reason: Flat. OR-chain associativity collapse via combine canonicalization is allocation-neutral; r_e4 not load-bearing for floor 12.

rejected_forms.reason: WORST regression (12 -> 37). Branchless-equivalent encoding via single unsigned compare moves codegen to entirely different shape ‚Äî emits sltu + nested branch tree, scheduling cascade through dispatch region.

rejected_forms.reason: Regression (12 -> 20). Top-level read extends mode's pseudo lifetime across entire clamp region; bumps its allocno priority enough to shift another pseudo's allocation, regressing 8 points.

Vetted per 6-test checklist: dead-store-on-one-path = YES, human-natural = NO, GCC-internals-justification = YES, permuter-found-only = YES.

rejected_forms.form: Initial var_v0_2 = arg0 before the if/else clamp (early-init + conditional overwrite)

rejected_forms.reason: Regression. Explicit goto-merge doesn't induce cc1 to emit a real park-merge insn; the merge label gets coalesced by jump-thread/CSE and the cascade gets WORSE (16 vs 12). (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: X-mask precompute (`var_v0 = var_v0_2 & 0xFFF;` before dispatch, narrow-mode overwrites)

The X-precompute happens between Y-precompute and the mode-dispatch and disturbs the bnez delay slot scheduling ‚Äî narrow-arm needs the overwrite, wide-arm uses the precompute, but the combined emission is 2 insns worse than the bare candidate. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: v6 (round 2): s32 lim_x = D_8009BE78 block-local (full halfword as s32 to avoid sign-extension) ‚Äî THE FIRST KNOWN LEVER that flips X-preserve pseudo to $a3

Also produces target's missing idx-16 `move a3, v0` park-merge. COST: lim_x's lifetime introduces extra early `move $3, $4` preserve and shifts pseudo 77 to $a1 (target $a2), netting +6 cascade diffs. THIS IS THE NEXT-SESSION BASELINE FOR DIRECTED PERMUTER. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)
